summarization.a: The figure displays multiple XRD spectra of SnS₂, HfS₂, ZrS₂,  films grown on mica, with intensity plotted against φ (°).

a.question: Does the figure only show in-plane scans of (10\bar{1}0)  reflections of the 2D material?
What is the preferential out-of-plane orientation for the materials?
a.answer: In terms of the out-of-plane orientation, all of these materials were strongly (0 0 0 1) oriented with FWHMs of 0.15°–0.17°

a.question: What domains show the lowest ntensity for SnS2, HfS2, and ZrS2 ?
a.answer:
In-plane, both 0°/60° and 30°/90° domains were observed for all of the materials, although in different amounts. For SnS2, the amount of the 30°/90°
domains was the lowest, whereas for ZrS2 nearly identical amounts of the 0°/60° and 30°/90° domains were observed. The intensity ratios of the 0°/60° to 30°/90° domains obtained by integrating the peak areas were approximately 4:1, 3:1, and 1:1 for SnS2, HfS2, and ZrS2, respectively. Furthermore,  ifferences in the FWHMs were observed. The 0°/60° domains of ZrS2 exhibited the smallest, although still quite large, mosaicity with a FWHM of approximately 9°compared to 14° and 19° for SnS2 and HfS2.

a.question: Can the in-plane disorder be minimized?
a.answer: In-plane FWHMs similar to SnS2, HfS2, and ZrS2  seen in figure  have been observed in several reports on vdW epitaxy in literature. obtained an in-plane. By further optimizing the process, the in-plane disorder can be decreased.
